c:I[2119,["2972","static/chunks/2972-9ebc76942f6ff386.js","570","static/chunks/570-b6ab106c455d39fe.js","3185","static/chunks/app/layout-e584c21e12f58b1f.js"],"Nav"]
d:I[376,["7601","static/chunks/app/error-00bcbcbf5966d127.js"],"default"]
2:T53a,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"How many calories should I eat to lose weight?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"To lose weight, eat 300-500 calories below your TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure). This creates a sustainable deficit of 0.5-1lb (0.25-0.5kg) per week. For faster loss, a 750-calorie deficit yields 1.5lb per week, but don't drop below 1,200 calories/day for women or 1,500 for men without medical supervision."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How many calories should I eat to build muscle?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"To build muscle, eat 200-300 calories above your TDEE. This small surplus provides energy for muscle growth while minimizing fat gain. Combine with adequate protein (1.6-2.2g/kg) and progressive resistance training. Expect to gain 0.25-0.5kg of muscle per month as a beginner, less as you advance."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How do I calculate my TDEE?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Calculate TDEE by first finding your BMR (Basal Metabolic Rate) using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, then multiply by an activity factor: Sedentary (×1.2), Lightly Active (×1.375), Moderately Active (×1.55), Very Active (×1.725), or Extremely Active (×1.9). Use our free TDEE calculator for instant results."}}]}7:{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}
6:["path","$7"]
9:{"d":"m12 5 7 7-7 7","key":"xquz4c"}

5:["$6","$8"]

Calculate TDEE, BMR, and macros all in one place."}],["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/tdee-calculator","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-6 py-3 bg-gradient-to-r from-orange-500 to-orange-600 text-white rounded-full font-medium hover:from-orange-600 hover:to-orange-700 transition-all","children":["Calculate My Calorie Needs",["$","$L3",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}],["path",{"d":"m12 5 7 7-7 7","key":"xquz4c"}]],"className":"lucide-arrow-right w-4 h-4"}]]}]]}]]}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose prose-lg max-w-none prose-headings:text-white prose-p:text-slate-400 prose-strong:text-white prose-li:text-slate-400","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"Quick Answer: Calories by Goal"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/40 rounded-xl p-6 not-prose my-6","children":["$","table",null,{"className":"w-full text-left","children":[["$","thead",null,{"children":["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-700","children":[["$","th",null,{"className":"pb-3 text-white font-semibold","children":"Goal"}],["$","th",null,{"className":"pb-3 text-white font-semibold","children":"Calorie Adjustment"}],["$","th",null,{"className":"pb-3 text-white font-semibold","children":"Expected Rate"}]]}]}],["$","tbody",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":[["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Fat Loss"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"TDEE - 300 to 500"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"0.5-1 lb/week"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Maintenance"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"= TDEE"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"No change"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Muscle Gain"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"TDEE + 200 to 300"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"0.25-0.5 lb/week"}]]}]]}]]}]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"What Are Calories?"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"A calorie is a unit of energy. The food you eat provides calories, and your body burns them through:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"BMR (Basal Metabolic Rate):"}]," Energy used at complete rest — about 60-70% of daily burn"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"TEF (Thermic Effect of Food):"}]," Energy used digesting food — about 10% of daily burn"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"NEAT (Non-Exercise Activity):"}]," Walking, fidgeting, standing — 15-30% of daily burn"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"EAT (Exercise Activity):"}]," Formal workouts — 5-15% of daily burn"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"How to Calculate Your TDEE"}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Step 1: Calculate BMR"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Use the Mifflin-St Jeor equation:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Men:"}]," BMR = (10 × weight in kg) + (6.25 × height in cm) - (5 × age) + 5"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Women:"}]," BMR = (10 × weight in kg) + (6.25 × height in cm) - (5 × age) - 161"]}]]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Step 2: Apply Activity Multiplier"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/40 rounded-xl p-6 not-prose my-6","children":["$","table",null,{"className":"w-full text-left","children":[["$","thead",null,{"children":["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-700","children":[["$","th",null,{"className":"pb-3 text-white font-semibold","children":"Activity Level"}],["$","th",null,{"className":"pb-3 text-white font-semibold","children":"Multiplier"}],["$","th",null,{"className":"pb-3 text-white font-semibold","children":"Description"}]]}]}],["$","tbody",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":[["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Sedentary"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"× 1.2"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Desk job, little exercise"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Lightly Active"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"× 1.375"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"1-3 days light exercise/week"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Moderately Active"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"× 1.55"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"3-5 days moderate exercise/week"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"className":"border-b border-slate-800/50","children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Very Active"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"× 1.725"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"6-7 days hard exercise/week"}]]}],["$","tr",null,{"children":[["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Extremely Active"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"× 1.9"}],["$","td",null,{"className":"py-3","children":"Physical job + hard training"}]]}]]}]]}]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Step 3: Adjust for Your Goal"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Lose weight:"}]," Subtract 300-500 calories from TDEE"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Maintain:"}]," Eat exactly your TDEE"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Build muscle:"}]," Add 200-300 calories to TDEE"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Example Calculation"}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Person:"}]," 30-year-old male, 75kg, 180cm, moderately active, wants to lose fat."]}],["$","ol",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"BMR = (10 × 75) + (6.25 × 180) - (5 × 30) + 5 = 1,730 calories"}],["$","li",null,{"children":["TDEE = 1,730 × 1.55 = ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"2,682 calories"}]]}],["$","li",null,{"children":["Fat loss target = 2,682 - 500 = ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"2,182 calories/day"}]]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Common Calorie Counting Mistakes"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Underestimating portion sizes:"}]," A \"handful\" of nuts is often 2-3 servings. Use a food scale for accuracy."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Forgetting liquid calories:"}]," Coffee drinks, smoothies, and alcohol add up fast."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Inconsistent tracking:"}]," Track every day, including weekends. One untracked day can undo a week of deficit."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Overestimating activity:"}]," Most people overestimate their activity level. When in doubt, choose the lower category."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Ignoring metabolic adaptation:"}]," As you lose weight, your TDEE decreases. Recalculate every 4-6 weeks."]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Related Tools"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/tdee-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"TDEE Calculator"}]," — Calculate your total daily energy expenditure"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/calorie-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Calorie Calculator"}]," — Find your maintenance and target calories"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/macro-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Macro Calculator"}]," — Split your calories into protein, carbs, and fat"]}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mt-16","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-8","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-6","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"How many calories should I eat to lose weight?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"To lose weight, eat 300-500 calories below your TDEE. This creates a sustainable deficit of 0.5-1lb (0.25-0.5kg) per week. For faster loss, a 750-calorie deficit yields 1.5lb per week, but don't drop below 1,200 calories/day for women or 1,500 for men without medical supervision."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"How many calories should I eat to build muscle?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"To build muscle, eat 200-300 calories above your TDEE. Combine with adequate protein (1.6-2.2g/kg) and progressive resistance training."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl Active (×1.9)."}]]}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-16 bg-gradient-to-br from-orange-500 to-orange-600 rounded-2xl p-8 md:p-12 text-center","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl
